MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Kirstin Wallace (Email: kirstin.wallace@lewisbrisbois.com) of the law offices of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard Smith LLP (Tel: 925.357.3328) appears as counsel for cross-defendants Watts Rebulator, Brasscraft Manufacturing)

Attorney, J.D. Turner (Email: JTurner@sellarlaw.com) of the law offices of SELLAR HAZARD & LUCIA (Tel: 925.938.1430) appears as counsel for Defendants and Cross-Complainants, CALIFORNIA HOMESTEAD ASSOCIATION and JAMES E. ROBERTS-OBAYASHI CORPORATION

Attorney, Eric Shapland (Email: eric.shapland@arnoldporter.com) of the law offices of Arnold & Porter (Tel: 213) 243-4000) appears as counsel for Cross-defendant/moving party, Kohler Co.

Attorney, Katherine M. (Kate) Wittlake (Email: kate.wittlake@faegredrinker.com) of the law offices of Faegre Drinker Biddle & Reath (Tel: 415.591.7529) appears as counsel for cross defendant Johnson Controls

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

CROSS DEFENDANT KOHLER CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is Granted in part and denied in part. Kohler Co.'s summary judgment motion on the cross-complaint by James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corp. (Builder) is denied.

Kohler's summary adjudication motion is granted in part and denied in part. Builder's opposition abandons its causes of action 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and 10. Summary adjudication is granted on them.

Cause of action 3 is for implied contractual indemnity, which "is available when two parties in a contractual relationship were both responsible for injuring a third party" and there is no indemnity clause in their contract. (Prince v. Pacific Gas & Electric Co. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 1151, 1159.) No contract between Kohler and Builder is claimed, so summary adjudication is granted on this cause.

Cause of action 2 is for equitable indemnity. Civil Code sec.910 et seq. regards prelitigation procedures in construction defect cases. Civil Code sec.916(e) provides: "Except with respect to any claims involving a repair actually conducted under this chapter, nothing in this subdivision shall be construed to relieve a product manufacturer of any liability under any cause of action brought by a claimant." Here, homeowners brought claims against Builder and Builder seeks equitable indemnity from Kohler under those claims. Product manufacturer Kohler points to no "repair actually conducted." (Id.) Thus, Builder's equitable indemnity cause of action is not barred. (Id.; Greystone Homes, Inc. v. Midtec, Inc. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1194, 1117-20 ("builder may bring an equitable indemnity action against a product manufacturer under the Act, seeking reimbursement for a homeowner's economic losses").) The cases Kohler cites involved direct homeowner suits against builders - not equitable indemnity claims - and are not contrary to Greystone. Summary adjudication is denied on this cause of action.

Kohler's notice of motion does not mention cause of action 8 (declaratory relief).

Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.

Defendant California Homestead Association'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Cochrane, Esq. 925-926-1200 of Hughes Gill Cochrane Tinetti for Plaintiff.
James Gilyard, Esq. 925-771-2561 of Sellar Hazard & Lucia for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues class action, statute of limitations. Plaintiff responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant California Homestead Association. James Roberts-Obayashi Corporations' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants California Homestead Association and James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corporation's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to Plaintiff's first cause of action is denied. A motion for judgment on the pleadings lies only for defects on the face of the complaint-extrinsic matters are improper. (Code Civ. Proc., 438(d).) As such, the Court cannot consider discovery admissions or other evidence controverting the pleadings. (Id.; see Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2018) 7:322.) Defendants' heavy reliance on such extrinsic evidence, including the expert report referred to in its moving papers and the numerous documents as to which it seeks judicial notice, is improper. Defendants' requests for judicial notice are denied, and Plaintiff's evidentiary objections to the attached documents are sustained.
Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to state a claim for a violation of the Right to Repair Act. (Civ. Code 895 et seq.) The complaint alleges violations of Civil Code 896, subd. (a)(14), subd. (a)(15), and subd. (g)(3)(A). (See Compl. 37.) Plaintiff does not solely seek recovery for an alleged defect in a manufactured product. (Civ. Code, 896(g)(3)(E); cf. Kohler Co. v. Superior Court (2018) Cal.App.5th 55, 63 ["A homeowner alleging that a manufactured product-such as a plumbing fixture-installed in her home is defective may bring a claim under the Act only if the allegedly defective product caused a violation of one of the standards set forth in section 896."]) Plaintiff's claims are not barred by the statute of limitations. (See Civ. Code, 941, subd. (a) ["Subject to certain exceptions, no action may be brought under Civil Code 895 et seq. more than 10 years after substantial completion of the development, but not later than 10 years after recordation of a valid notice of completion."].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
